TextENGNew: ...This is one of those special pieces born from the process that gave rise to the Onikakushi, Watanagashi, and Tatarigoroshi fragments.
The story of a sad tragedy that fell upon Natsumi Kimiyoshi — a girl who carried the blood of the Three Families, yet should have been living a life with no connection to Hinamizawa.
changeReason: OLD says she was 'trying to live a life outside of Hinamizawa' (implying active effort), while JPN 'はずの' and NEW say she 'should have been living a life with no connection to Hinamizawa' (implying expectation/assumption, not active effort). NEW also adds 'The story of' matching JPN 'お話.'

TextENGNew: The trigger was nothing more than a handful of small misunderstandings.
But as they accumulated, all the frustration and anxiety she had long kept buried exploded at once — and she found herself consumed by a murderous impulse she never could have imagined.
changeReason: OLD omits the JPN element '予想だにしていなかった' (never could have imagined), which NEW restores as 'she never could have imagined.' Also, 'bloodlust' vs 'impulse' — JPN '殺意' means murderous intent/impulse, not bloodlust.

TextENGNew: From the moment she arrived in her new town, she had secretly harbored an inferiority complex toward those around her.
A slight mismatch in conversation, a difference in values, or a perceived inequality in ability — these small things are enough to make a person feel estranged and alone.
changeReason: OLD renders JPN '話題のズレ' (mismatch in conversation topics) as 'sense of not fitting in' and '能力の優劣' (inequality in ability) as 'feeling of inferiority' — different nouns/concepts. NEW's 'mismatch in conversation' and 'inequality in ability' are closer to the specific causes listed in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: I wonder — have you yourself ever had such an experience...?
And yet, from the end of this tragedy, one small hope was spun out — one we had never anticipated.
It was something slightly different from a fragment — something small and ephemeral that, at first, could not even be called a lump at all.
And yet, as time passed, it gradually came to hold a certain power.
